Empires & Puzzles Costumed Doxan Review: The Elemental Hacker
Doxan traded his lasers for data streams and returned with vengeance. With vastly improved stats and a devastating burn, the Exiled Plasma Hacker is ready to delete Nature heroes from the server.
Costumed Doxan: Hacking the Elemental Code

Released in September 2025, Costumed Doxan (The "Exiled Plasma Hacker") is the modern reinvention of a hero who had fallen behind the curve. By equipping this costume, Doxan transforms from an outdated 2023 sniper into a statistically relevant 2025 powerhouse. With a massive stat boost and significantly enhanced damage multipliers, he reclaims his role as a premier anti-Nature assassin. This review focuses on the Costume version, which is now the definitive way to play this hero.

The costume shifts Doxan from a generic sci-fi gladiator to a "Matrix-meets-Cyberpunk" hacker. He sports data-stream visuals, a more tactical outfit, and "Dual Data Blades" that look like they are slicing through the game's code itself. The visual upgrade matches the stat upgrade—he looks sharper, more dangerous, and decidedly more modern. The "glitch" effects in the artwork are a nice touch that hints at his disruptive potential.
This is the most critical section. The costume propels Doxan from the minor leagues to the majors.
. This is a ~40% increase in base striking power.
Defense and Health see similar massive gains (Health from 1705 to 2483).

Costumed Doxan has legitimate 2025 stats. He is no longer a glass cannon; he is a reinforced titanium cannon. He can survive a hit from modern defenders and has the attack stat to penetrate modern defense values.
"Dual Data Blades" takes everything the original skill did and turns the volume up to 11.

The mechanic remains: hit a target, and all Nature enemies take minor damage and the full burn. Against a team with 2-3 Nature heroes, a single fire of this skill deals thousands of damage collectively.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities

The Super Elemental family bonus remains a nice-to-have, spawning power gems. The passive resistance/damage modification is enhanced in the costume, making him even deadlier against Green and tankier against Blue.

Unlike some modern snipers, he doesn't bypass defensive buffs or dispel (unless you rely on Ranger talent, which he lost—he is now Rogue).
A cleanser can neutralize a large portion of his damage potential.

A solid pick for any red stack, bringing fast speed and high damage.
Kong reflects blue (protecting Doxan), Gestalt hits randoms, and Doxan finishes them off.
C-Marjana provides Elemental Defense Down, amplifying Doxan's already absurd damage.
If the enemy cleanses the 1504 burn, you lose a lot of value.
He is a single-target hitter (mostly). A Taunt hero can absorb his hit and protect the Green allies from the splash damage.
If you pull the costume, you have won the jackpot. He is easy to use (point and shoot) and will be your strongest red hero for a long time. Prioritize leveling him immediately.

Costumed Doxan is worth the mats. Even if you have other strong reds, his tile damage against Titans alone justifies the investment. In wars, he is a reliable delete button. He essentially replaces older snipers like Marjana or Gefjon in your roster.
"Is Costumed Doxan worth Summoning For? Yes, absolutely."

"Looks like a great flank to a green tank."

- The hype was real for this release.

Costumed Doxan is the perfect example of how a costume can redeem a hero. He went from "meh" to "monster." He hits hard, burns harder, and dodges the haters (literally). Whether you are fighting a Nature Titan or a annoying Green tank, Doxan is the tool for the job.
A top-tier sniper who finally lives up to his sci-fi potential.
Doxan gets a massive upgrade with his 'Exiled Plasma Hacker' costume. We review his new stats, enhanced burn damage, and whether he is now a top-tier sniper.
